title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Biden made a surprise appearance at Boston's St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast organized by the Irish American Partnership.
Biden's speech focused on Irish American history and current immigration challenges, linking past experiences to today's issues.
Governor Maura Healey joined Biden in emphasizing the shared immigrant heritage and reflecting on past treatment of immigrants.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured in a deck collapse at a Brighton party, police reported.
The deck railing broke off unexpectedly, causing the students to fall from the second floor onto concrete.
Police noted rotting wood and corroded nails on the deck, with painted surfaces possibly concealing the damage.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots safety Craig Woodson earned a $1,149,910 performance bonus after a strong rookie season, adding to his $840,000 base salary.
Woodson started 15 games with 950 snaps in 2025, leading the Patriots' defense in plays and recording 79 tackles.
In the postseason, Woodson excelled with 30 tackles and six pass breakups, including a standout Super Bowl performance.

title: Roman Anthony wears Charlie McAvoy’s USA Olympics jersey before WBC final
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony wore Charlie McAvoy's Team USA hockey jersey before the World Baseball Classic final.
Team USA baseball players wore game-worn US Men's Hockey Team jerseys from the 2026 Winter Olympics to the WBC final.
Anthony, a key player for Team USA, has a .318 average with two home runs and seven RBIs in the WBC.

title: Ex-Celtic Marcus Smart recalls 2018 incident that almost made him lose his right hand
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Marcus Smart punched a picture frame in 2018, resulting in a severe hand injury requiring 20 stitches.
The injury cost Smart 11 games, and glass remains in his hand, risking potential permanent damage.
Smart now plays for the Lakers after stints with Memphis and Washington following his time with the Celtics.

title: Boston Marathon runner honors sister’s recovery from severe crash
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Michael Chiang runs the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation to honor his sister's recovery.
Chiang's sister, Jennifer, relearned to walk and ran a 5K after a severe car accident.
Jennifer's recovery included spinal surgery and rehabilitation at Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.

title: Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The body believed to be Jason Thomas, missing since December, was recovered from Lake Quannapowitt in Wakefield.
Police do not suspect foul play; the Medical Examiner will confirm identity and cause of death.
Thomas, a Novartis scientist, disappeared on December 12 and was last seen near Murray and Chestnut streets.

title: Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts' online casino bill, H. 4431, sent to study by a 11-0 committee vote, effectively halting progress this year.
The bill aimed to allow digital slot machines and blackjack regulated by the Massachusetts Gaming Commission under Chapter 23O.
Supporters cited transparency and safety benefits, while opponents warned of increased competition for casinos and addiction risks.

title: Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus crashed into parked cars and a home in Medford on March 8; driver suspected of being drowsy.
The 40-year-old driver faces charges of negligent operation, failing to stop at a red light, and a marked lanes violation.
Investigators ruled out alcohol and intoxicating substances; temperatures during the incident ranged between 49 and 51 degrees.

title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Erik Mogensen purchased Black Mountain, N.H., shifting focus from community co-op to a research lab for independent ski areas.
Mogensen, Indy Pass director, aims to solve challenges like staffing shortages and costs that threaten independent ski areas.
Black Mountain, N.H., surpassed last year's attendance with Mogensen's leadership, now hosting record-breaking ski seasons.
